close_reason: Correct: hand-rolled JSON scanner avoids serde_json dependency for field extraction
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:121-160: The crate has two parallel hand-rolled JSON scanners (char-based and byte-based) totalling ~700 LOC. A contributor will reasonably ask 'why not use serde_json?' — and the answer is in the long comment at lib.rs:144-156 plus the SecretValue doc comment.

Defense reason: serde_json deserializes every field value into a non-Zeroizing String. Even after the parsed Value is dropped, those allocations sit in the heap un-zeroed until the allocator reuses the page. For a JSON secret bundle ({"username":"...","password":"..."}), parsing with serde_json to extract one field leaks the *other* fields into unzeroed memory.

The hand-rolled scanner avoids this by skipping non-target fields without allocating their content (json_skip_value never reads the value into a String).

Caveat: the scanner currently has its OWN zeroization gap (avkc.1) when building the target value. That bug must be fixed, but it does not invalidate the design choice — fixing avkc.1 keeps the entire system zeroize-clean, while serde_json never could.

Future contributor: if you suggest 'just use serde_json', point them at avkc.1 and this comment.
title: DEFEND: Hand-rolled JSON scanner over serde_json
